\noindent The results on page 119 are obtained with the
\ttblue{FSaudit} package, first by creating an
attribute object, filling it with the parameters
of the test, and then applying the
\ttblue{size()} function.
The relevant parameters are the significance level
\ttblue{alpha}, the number of deviations in the population
$M$ \ttblue{popdev}, the population size $N$ \ttblue{popn},
and the critical region \ttblue{c}.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
mySample = att_sample(alpha=0.1, popdev=60, popn=1200, 
c=0).size()
mySample.n
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 45
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
